name: org.machanism.machai:ghostwriter
description: Machai Ghostwriter is a Java 8 command-line documentation and automation engine that scans a project directory tree (filesystem-only; no builds or dependency resolution during scanning) and executes GenAI-powered workflows over matching files, using a project-layout model for multi-module projects (child modules processed before the parent). It supports two primary processing modes: (1) Guidance mode (GuidanceProcessor), which discovers file-format reviewers via Java ServiceLoader (Reviewer SPI) and extracts embedded "@guidance:" directives from supported file types (Java including package-info.java, Markdown, HTML/HTM/XML, TypeScript, Python, PlantUML, and a special "@guidance.txt" convention) to produce per-file prompt fragments; and (2) Act mode (ActProcessor), which executes predefined TOML-based prompt templates ("acts") loaded from built-in classpath resources (/acts/*.toml) and optionally a user-provided directory or HTTP(S) URL, with inheritance via "basedOn" and support for prologue/epilogue sequences. Ghostwriter provides flexible scanning semantics implemented by AbstractFileProcessor/AIFileProcessor: include matching uses Java PathMatcher with glob:/regex: patterns, supports a scanDir concept (relative to the project or absolute within project root), configurable exclusion patterns (exact relative paths or glob/regex), default excludes for common build/tooling directories via ProjectLayout, optional non-recursive processing (disable module recursion), and optional concurrent module processing with a configurable thread pool and safe shutdown timeout. Prompt orchestration is performed by AIFileProcessor: it creates a GenAI provider via GenaiProviderManager using a configured provider/model key, applies function tools via FunctionToolsLoader, sets the provider working directory to the project root, composes prompts that include a structured project layout metadata block (OS, project/module identifiers, source/test/docs folders, child projects, and project-relative file path), expands instruction/prompt inputs line-by-line including URL and file: inclusions, and optionally logs the composed provider inputs per processed file into .machai/docs-inputs for auditing. The CLI entry point (Ghostwriter) uses Apache Commons CLI, reads configuration from gw.properties (and system properties like gw.home/gw.config), supports interactive stdin entry for instructions/guidance/acts, and exposes options for model selection, scan patterns, excludes, instructions, default guidance, act execution and act directory, concurrency, and input logging. The distribution includes built-in acts such as help, commit, release-notes, sonar-fix, grype-fix, unit-tests, task, and assembly; and registers Ghostwriter-specific function tools (ActFunctionTools) that expose build_in_list_acts and load_act_details at runtime. Primary integrations are Machai project-layout and Machai genai-client provider orchestration; TOML parsing uses org.tomlj; logging is via SLF4J/Logback.

constructors.description: Creates the Ghostwriter CLI orchestrator with a required GenAI provider/model identifier (e.g., OpenAI:gpt-5.1) and a configured processor implementation (GuidanceProcessor or ActProcessor).

examples.description: Create a Guidance-mode processor and a Ghostwriter CLI instance programmatically.

constructors.description: Creates a GuidanceProcessor that scans the filesystem, uses ServiceLoader-discovered reviewers to extract @guidance directives per supported file type, and dispatches prompts to the configured GenAI provider. Supports module processing, path matching, excludes, and optional default prompt behavior.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.PropertiesConfigurator cfg = new org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.PropertiesConfigurator();
org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.GuidanceProcessor processor = new org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.GuidanceProcessor(new java.io.File("."), "OpenAI:gpt-5.1", cfg);
examples.description: Construct a GuidanceProcessor with a root directory, provider/model id, and configurator.

constructors.description: Creates an ActProcessor that loads TOML-based act templates (built-in and/or user-defined via directory or URL), supports inheritance via basedOn, applies act properties to runtime settings (instructions/default prompt/threads/excludes/nonRecursive), and executes the act against matching files and directories.

examples.description: Construct an ActProcessor and select an act (with optional prompt text) to apply.

constructors.description: Creates a processor that can compose prompts with project layout metadata and execute a configured GenAI provider, including optional function tools and input logging.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.PropertiesConfigurator cfg = new org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.PropertiesConfigurator();
org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.AIFileProcessor p = new org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.AIFileProcessor(new java.io.File("."), cfg, "OpenAI:gpt-5.1");
examples.description: Create a base AIFileProcessor (typically used via subclasses) with a provider/model id.

features.name: Scan documents with directory/glob/regex matching
features.signature: void scanDocuments(java.io.File projectDir, java.lang.String scanDir) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Scans a project root using either a raw relative directory name or a glob:/regex: PathMatcher expression. Enforces that absolute scan paths must reside under the project root, sets scanDir/pathMatcher for downstream match logic, and triggers filesystem traversal with default excludes and optional custom excludes. Also initializes FunctionToolsLoader configuration before scanning.

examples.description: Scan only Java source files using a glob matcher.

features.name: Act loading with inheritance (basedOn) from classpath and directory/URL
features.signature: static void loadAct(java.lang.String name, java.util.Map<java.lang.String, java.lang.Object> properties, java.lang.String actsLocation) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Loads TOML act definitions from built-in classpath resources (/acts/<name>.toml) and optionally a user-defined directory or HTTP(S) URL, supports inheritance via basedOn (recursive parent loading), and merges dotted TOML keys into a properties map where child properties override or extend parent values.

examples.snippet:
java.util.Map<java.lang.String, java.lang.Object> props = new java.util.HashMap<java.lang.String, java.lang.Object>();
org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.ActProcessor.loadAct("help", props, "./acts");
examples.description: Load an act named 'help' and merge inherited properties.

features.name: Guidance extraction via Reviewer SPI (ServiceLoader)
features.signature: java.lang.String parseFile(java.io.File projectDir, java.io.File file) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Selects a Reviewer implementation based on file extension (loaded via java.util.ServiceLoader) and extracts @guidance prompt fragments for supported formats. Returns null for unsupported files, non-files, or when no guidance is present.

examples.description: Extract a guidance fragment from a Java source file if it contains @guidance: directives.

features.name: Provider prompt orchestration with project layout metadata and optional input logging
features.signature: java.lang.String process(org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout projectLayout, java.io.File file, java.lang.String instructions, java.lang.String prompt) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Creates a GenAI provider using the configured model, applies registered function tools, sets working directory to the project root, composes a prompt that includes a structured project layout description plus task/guidance/act text, supports URL/file: expansion for instruction/prompt lines, optionally logs provider inputs per file under .machai/docs-inputs, and performs the provider request.

examples.description: Send a composed prompt to the configured provider for a specific file.

features.name: Built-in act listing and act details function tools
features.description: Registers Ghostwriter-specific function tools into the provider, including build_in_list_acts (lists built-in act templates from the distribution) and load_act_details (loads an act's effective/built-in/custom definition), enabling act discovery and inspection by the GenAI provider.

examples.description: Register act-related function tools into a provider.

customizations.name: Reviewer SPI
customizations.description: Provide custom file-format guidance extraction by implementing Reviewer and registering it via Java ServiceLoader (META-INF/services/org.machanism.machai.gw.reviewer.Reviewer).

customizations.name: FunctionTools SPI
customizations.description: Extend provider capabilities by implementing FunctionTools and registering it via ServiceLoader; Ghostwriter contributes ActFunctionTools to expose act discovery/inspection tools.

customizations.name: Acts directory/URL
customizations.description: Extend or override built-in act templates by providing an external acts location via configuration key gw.acts or CLI option --acts; acts may be loaded from a directory or an HTTP(S) URL and can inherit using basedOn.

customizations.name: Exclude patterns
customizations.description: Customize scan exclusions using gw.excludes (comma-separated) or programmatically via setExcludes(String[]); supports exact relative paths and glob:/regex: patterns.

customizations.name: Concurrency and shutdown timeout
customizations.description: Control concurrent module processing with gw.threads and configure module worker pool shutdown timeout via setModuleThreadTimeoutMinutes(long).

customizations.name: Instruction expansion via URL and file: references
customizations.description: Provide multi-line instructions or prompts where each non-blank line may be a URL (http/https) to include remote content or a file: reference (supports system property substitution) to include local file content.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.gw.reviewer.Reviewer
studs.description: SPI for components that scan project files and extract @guidance instructions to generate normalized prompt fragments for Ghostwriter.

examples.description: Skeleton for a custom Reviewer implementation.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools
studs.description: SPI for registering function tools into a GenAI provider; Ghostwriter contributes ActFunctionTools to expose act discovery and act inspection tools at runtime.

examples.description: Skeleton for a custom FunctionTools implementation.
